accurate.why: All claims match the bill text and the contextual description of Fourteenth Amendment Section 2 is factually accurate.

no_hallucinations.why: Added context about Section 2 addressing reduced congressional representation for denying male inhabitants the vote is verified and correct.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill as a proposed constitutional amendment establishing a fundamental right to vote.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly notes the repeal of the 'or other crime' phrase from Section 2 of the Fourteenth Amendment and accurately explains that provision.

exceptions_conditions.why: Captures the compelling-interest/least-restrictive-means standard governing denial or abridgment of the right to vote.

effective_dates.why: Correctly notes the ratification requirement of two-thirds of Congress and three-fourths of state legislatures.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers all four sections: the right to vote, strict scrutiny standard, the repeal, and the congressional enforcement power.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive with no editorializing.

correct_entities.why: Correctly identifies the Fourteenth Amendment, Congress, and the states' role in ratification.

correct_figures.why: The two-thirds and three-fourths ratification thresholds match the bill and the Constitution.
